The key is to strictly follow the sequence: **interest first, then transaction** at each year-end.

| Step | Calculation | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| **Start** | | **$10,000.00** |
| Year 1 interest | $10,000.00 × 1.08 | $10,800.00 |
| Year 1 withdraw | $10,800.00 − $500 | **$10,300.00** |
| Year 2 interest | $10,300.00 × 1.08 | $11,124.00 |
| Year 2 withdraw | $11,124.00 − $800 | **$10,324.00** |
| Year 3 interest | $10,324.00 × 1.08 | $11,149.92 |
| Year 3 deposit | $11,149.92 + $300 | **$11,449.92** |
| Year 4 interest | $11,449.92 × 1.08 | $12,365.9136 |
| Year 4 withdraw | $12,365.91 − $1,200 | **$11,165.91** |

**The account balance immediately after the Year 4 transaction is $11,166.**
